responseScale.displayRule: Every response option MUST display its number together with its verbal anchor. Numeric-only option buttons are non-conforming (v1.0 field defect: attention checks referenced anchors respondents could not see).

items.text: I enjoy teaching someone a new skill.
I enjoy repairing broken objects with my hands.
I enjoy organizing information into clear systems.
I enjoy studying information to find out why something happens.
I enjoy convincing others to support an idea.
I enjoy creating original work, such as a design, a story, or a piece of music.
I like using tools or machines to complete a task.
I like helping people find a way through their problems.
I like solving problems that require deep thinking.
I like checking details to make sure everything is correct.
I like finding new ways to express an idea.
I like taking the lead when a group needs direction.
I enjoy exploring new ideas and discoveries in any subject.
items.v10Text: I enjoy reading about science, technology, or new discoveries.

items.text: I enjoy building things I can touch when they are finished.
Seeing a person grow because of my support makes my work feel meaningful.
I work best when I have a clear plan and a fixed schedule.
To show that you are reading each statement, select answer 4 ('Agree') here.
items.v10Text: To show that you are reading each statement, please choose "Agree" for this one.

items.text: Working toward a clear goal against a challenge brings out my best effort.
items.v10Text: Competition brings out my best effort.

items.text: I do my best work in places with freedom and few fixed rules.
I feel comfortable in a workshop, on a work site, or outdoors.
I prefer work where I am not responsible for anyone else's progress.
items.v10Text: I do my best work when I am alone for most of the day.

items.text: Completing a task exactly as planned gives me satisfaction.
I like work settings where careful analysis matters more than speed.
I am more comfortable supporting a plan than deciding it.
items.v10Text: I prefer to follow a leader rather than be the leader.

items.text: Making something beautiful gives me energy.
I like jobs where I can see the physical results of my work at the end of the day.
I prefer workplaces where people cooperate closely.
Digging into the details of a problem does not appeal to me.
items.v10Text: I lose interest when a task requires long, detailed analysis.

items.text: I enjoy jobs where every day is different and unplanned.
I enjoy work environments where things move quickly.
I prefer tasks with one correct answer over tasks with many possible answers.
Working with physical materials gives me energy.
For this statement, select answer 1 ('Strongly disagree').
items.v10Text: For this statement, please select "Strongly disagree."

items.text: I enjoy giving my full attention when someone shares a personal difficulty.
items.v10Text: I enjoy listening carefully when someone shares a personal difficulty.

items.text: Understanding how something truly works gives me deep satisfaction.
I enjoy keeping records accurate and up to date.
I like being the person who starts a new project.
I enjoy imagining things that do not exist yet.
Working with physical objects appeals to me less than other kinds of work.
items.v10Text: I would rather work with ideas than with physical objects.

items.text: Helping another person with a problem gives me energy.
items.v10Text: Conversations with people give me energy.

items.text: I like showing another person how to do something new.
I keep asking questions until I fully understand a topic.
I would rather improvise than follow a step-by-step plan.
Trying to persuade people drains my energy.
I prefer work where nothing is left open to my imagination.
items.v10Text: I work best when every step of my work is decided in advance.

items.text: I feel proud when I fix something that was not working.
I like roles where my main task is to support other people's growth.
I feel energized when I face a complicated problem.
This is a quality check. Select the middle answer, 3 ('Neutral').
items.v10Text: This is a quality check. Please choose "Neutral" for this statement.

items.text: I prefer a stable job with clear duties over a job that changes all the time.
Winning support for my idea after working hard for it excites me.
items.v10Text: Winning an important agreement gives me a strong feeling of excitement.

items.text: I am drawn to work where personal style and taste matter.
Spending the whole day working with my hands would not suit me.
items.v10Text: A full day of physical, hands-on work leaves me feeling drained.

items.text: A full day of helping people with personal problems would exhaust me.
I prefer a quick practical answer over a deep investigation.
I enjoy reviewing work carefully to find small errors.
Clear rules and procedures help me feel confident at work.
I avoid situations where my results are compared with others' results.
items.v10Text: I prefer a calm, predictable role over a competitive one.

items.text: I feel full of energy when I am inventing something new.
I enjoy taking devices apart to learn how they work.
I enjoy explaining difficult things in a way others can understand.
I like work that lets me spend a long time investigating a single question.
Keeping detailed records all day would drain my energy.
items.v10Text: Careful, detailed paperwork quickly drains my energy.

items.text: I would enjoy being responsible for the success of a business or a team.
Work that depends on my imagination tires me out.
items.v10Text: Work that asks me to create something from nothing drains my energy.
